other_observers: Bill Maynard; We found this bird.
specific_location: Sailboard launching area at Pueblo Reservoir
description: Bill Maynard and I were at Sailboard launching area, about to leave, when I spotted a loon that appeared to be small. After looking at it in the scopes, we could tell it was a small loon, with a thin gray ish colored bill, which the bird held up tilted. The bird was light gray overall. The head and back were gray, with a whitish colored face. The bird was loon shaped and smaller and lighter colored then the Common Loon we had just seen. The bird was also lighter gray then the two Pacific Loons that we saw after this bird.Behaviors: Swimming on the large reservoir.Call: nonePlumage: basic plumage
